# A/B side-mapping live test -- ground truth

Fixtures for `server/tests/onelineSideMappingLive.test.js`, generated by
`gen_test_onelines.py` in this folder (reuses the existing `sld.py` one-line
generator so these look like the same realistic diagrams used for demo data).

Purpose: validate the 2026-07-23 fix to `normSide()` in
`server/lib/arcFlashExtract.ts` (commit `6e2a812`) against the REAL extraction
engine (real AI calls) on realistic input, not just the hand-built unit test
in `arcFlashExtractSide.test.js`.

## 01_northfield_dc_train_ab.pdf -- Northfield Data Center, 2N
Pre-existing supported form ("TRAIN A" / "TRAIN B") -- regression baseline,
should have passed even before the fix.

| Bus | Expected side | Why |
|---|---|---|
| SWGR-MAIN | null | not labeled with a side |
| SWGR-A | A | labeled "TRAIN A" |
| PDU-A1 | A | labeled "TRAIN A" |
| RACK ROW A1 | null | deepest leaf, deliberately left unlabeled |
| SWGR-B | B | labeled "TRAIN B" |
| PDU-B1 | B | labeled "TRAIN B" |
| RACK ROW B1 | null | deepest leaf, deliberately left unlabeled |

## 02_meridian_health_left_right.pdf -- Meridian Health Data Hall, N+1
Bare "LEFT" / "RIGHT" -- the exact gap the fix closes.

| Bus | Expected side | Why |
|---|---|---|
| SWGR-MAIN | null | not labeled with a side |
| SWGR-L | A | labeled "LEFT" |
| PDU-L1 | A | labeled "LEFT" |
| RACK ROW L1 | null | deepest leaf, deliberately left unlabeled |
| SWGR-R | B | labeled "RIGHT" |
| PDU-R1 | B | labeled "RIGHT" |
| RACK ROW R1 | null | deepest leaf, deliberately left unlabeled |

## 03_riverside_industrial_leftside.pdf -- Riverside Industrial Plant
"LEFT SIDE" / "RIGHT SIDE" -- fix variant, different industry (pump feed, not
a data center) and different equipment (MCC/VFD/motor, not PDU/rack).

| Bus | Expected side | Why |
|---|---|---|
| SWGR-MAIN | null | not labeled with a side |
| MCC-1 | A | labeled "LEFT SIDE" |
| MCC-2 | B | labeled "RIGHT SIDE" |

## How to run
Makes real, billed AI calls -- never runs by default.

    RUN_LIVE_AI_TEST=1 npx jest onelineSideMappingLive --silent=false

## What a pass means / doesn't mean
A pass means: on these 3 realistic documents, the real extraction pipeline
(real AI read + normSide()) correctly recognized every side variant the fix
was meant to add, and correctly left unlabeled buses null rather than
guessing. It does NOT mean the AI model will always get this right on every
possible real-world document -- LLM extraction has irreducible variance.
Treat this as evidence, not a formal guarantee.
